findings.description: login() builds the SQL query via f-string interpolation of the user-supplied email, allowing trivial SQL injection and authentication bypass (e.g. email = ' OR '1'='1' --).

validate_session has inverted expiry logic: it deletes/rejects sessions whose expires_at is still in the future (valid) and returns user_id for already-expired sessions, so expired tokens are accepted and valid tokens are killed on first use.
rotate_token relies on the broken validate_session, so sliding renewal only succeeds for expired/invalid tokens and fails for live ones, inheriting the inverted-expiry defect.
LOCKOUT_MINUTES is declared but never used: failed_attempts is only reset on a successful login, so once it reaches MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS the account is locked permanently with no time-based unlock.

find_user_by_email runs SELECT * FROM users, materializes every user into objects, then linear-scans in Python instead of filtering with a WHERE clause, giving O(n) time and unbounded memory per lookup.
Unknown-email path returns before any password hashing while valid emails incur a PBKDF2 computation, creating a timing side-channel for user/email enumeration.

Sessions are stored only in a per-instance in-memory dict, so they are not shared across processes/workers and are lost on restart, breaking validation in any multi-process or load-balanced deployment.
PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 uses 100_000 iterations, well below the OWASP-recommended ~600_000 for this algorithm, weakening resistance to offline brute-force.
The read of failed_attempts and the increment UPDATE are not performed in a single atomic transaction, so concurrent failed logins can race and undercount attempts, weakening lockout enforcement.
datetime.utcnow() is deprecated as of Python 3.12 and returns a naive datetime; timezone-aware datetime.now(timezone.utc) should be used (also lines 105, 147).

On wrong-password the failed_attempts increment is committed but no lockout-timestamp is recorded, so combined with the unused LOCKOUT_MINUTES there is no audit of when lockout began for later unlock logic.
